titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in China ( (Stuart) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in China ( Stuart ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 1103. Available information indicates Communist forces Manchuria have assumed initiative and mounted sizable offensive from east and west against Changchun area. Although Changchun airfield under attack from Communist militia units night May 17 and Communist cavalry patrols operating within 7 kilometers of city, it seems unlikely at this juncture that Communists have intention or capability of occupying Changchun. It seems probable that Communists will endeavor harass Government line communication and supply south of Changchun with intent cause further attrition Government strength which may eventually force Government withdrawal southward.

Well coordinated plan is indicated by large scale raid Changli area May 18 (reference Embassy telegram 1088, May 19Not printed.). Possibility also exists that interdiction rail line Changli is forerunner to attack on major Government supply base for Manchuria at Hulutao.
